problem: George is about to get a certain amount of change less than one dollar from the cash register. If he gets the most quarters possible and the rest in pennies, he would need to receive 3 pennies to meet the amount. If he gets the most dimes possible and the rest in pennies, he would need to receive 8 pennies to meet the amount. What is the sum, in cents, of the possible amounts of change that he is trying to get?

classified_questions.question: What is the maximum value of quarters that George can receive if he wants to get the most quarters possible and the rest in pennies?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the remainder when the amount of change George wants to get, after receiving the maximum value of quarters, is divided by 1 cent?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the possible amounts of change, in cents, that George needs if he receives the maximum value of quarters and still needs 3 pennies?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the maximum value of dimes that George can receive if he wants to get the most dimes possible and the rest in pennies?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the remainder when the amount of change George wants to get, after receiving the maximum value of dimes, is divided by 1 cent?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the possible amounts of change, in cents, that George needs if he receives the maximum value of dimes and still needs 8 pennies?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the possible amounts of change that George is trying to get, considering the amounts he needs after receiving the maximum value of quarters and dimes?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the sum, in cents, of the possible amounts of change that George is trying to get?
classified_questions.label: b
